title: toxicodendron rehmannianum
intro: Toxicodendron rehmannianum, also known as poison sumac, is a plant species that belongs to the Anacardiaceae family and is native to the southeastern United States. The plant contains urushiol, a toxic compound that causes skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. Despite its toxic properties, poison sumac has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Poison sumac can be prepared as a tincture, decoction, or salve, and is often used in small quantities under the guidance of a healthcare professional to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
